title: compass project config
description: Schema for .claude/compass.yml — the single source of project config. Validated by /setup; powers editor autocomplete via the yaml-language-server comment at the top of compass.yml.

package_manager.description: JS package manager. Used for install when install_cmd is blank.

install_cmd.description: Custom install command for any stack, e.g. uv sync. Blank = use package_manager.

dev_port.description: Dev server port. Worktrees get dev_port + N.

test_policy.description: When tests are written for logic-bearing tasks in /compass:implement: first = test-first TDD (RED→GREEN); after = implement then write the test; none = no forced test. UI/glue/config tasks never force a test.

type_check_cmd.description: Type check, e.g. npm run typecheck. Blank if not used.

worktree_prefix.description: Worktree sibling prefix, e.g. ../my-project- (siblings of the main dir).

db_file.description: Optional file DB copied per worktree (file/SQLite only), e.g. myapp.db.

worktree_setup_cmd.description: Hook run in the new worktree after install (WT_NAME/WT_DIR/WT_BRANCH/WT_PORT exported).

autonomy_mode.description: CI behaviour. Only used if the CI workflow is installed. See AUTONOMY.md.

ci_review_provider.description: Which LLM runs the CI review. Secret per provider: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY / OPENAI_API_KEY / GEMINI_API_KEY.

tracker.description: Active issue tracker. 'none' = stories live only in .work/stories/. Set by /compass:setup-tracker.

tracker_get_issue_tool.description: MCP tool to fetch an issue by ID, e.g. mcp__linear-server__get_issue. Used by /compass:context. Blank or tracker=none means no issue loading.

tracker_create_issue_tool.description: MCP tool to create an issue, e.g. mcp__linear-server__save_issue. Used by /compass:create-stories.

tracker_get_team_tool.description: MCP tool to fetch the team/project for issue creation, e.g. mcp__linear-server__get_team. Some trackers (Azure DevOps) use a project name instead — leave blank.

description.description: One paragraph — what this project does. Filled by /setup.
